claim: Agents save yak-shaving work by eliminating dependency chains and installation issues, which is underappreciated compared to raw productivity gains
actionable: Use agents to handle repetitive setup and dependency resolution tasks; measure agent ROI by time saved on blocking dependencies, not just output speed

claim: Non-technical team members can learn to prompt agents effectively by observing how humans communicate; no manual instructions needed if communication is natural
actionable: Train non-technical users by example and natural language; let them interact with agents conversationally without formal documentation

claim: Agent feedback loops and reduced waiting time increase employee satisfaction and motivation, leading to more work being completed without explicit management pressure
actionable: Prioritize real-time agent feedback and minimize blocking delays; measure productivity gains through engagement metrics alongside output metrics

claim: MCPs (Model Context Protocols) and coding agents are becoming essential for managing conference-scale problems like speaker coordination and data sync across multiple systems
actionable: Use agents with MCP integrations for data management tasks; let agents handle routine ETL and synchronization across disconnected systems

claim: Agent-driven automation enables small teams to manage large events; a team of 9 people using agents can manage 1000-person conferences and scale to 6000-person events
actionable: Adopt agent-based workflows for high-volume coordination tasks; measure scalability by team-size-to-attendance ratio instead of headcount alone

claim: The future requires agent infrastructure that supports wikis and knowledge management tools for better code and team collaboration, not just end-to-end agents
actionable: Build agent systems that integrate with knowledge bases and wiki tools; don't force all intelligence into the agent loop itself

claim: Agent infrastructure must be designed for business continuity; employee concerns about agent implementation deserve respect and careful planning, not dismissal
actionable: When rolling out agent systems, identify and systematically reduce top employee concerns rather than dismissing objections as resistance
